name: Buttplug.io MCP Server
what_it_does: Buttplug.io MCP server enabling AI agents to control intimate/haptic devices supported by the Buttplug.io open-source protocol — connecting to Bluetooth LE and other supported devices, controlling vibration patterns and intensity, and integrating haptic feedback into agent-driven interactive experiences.
use_cases: Controlling haptic/vibration devices from interactive AI agents
Creating synchronized haptic feedback for interactive experiences
Building accessible haptic interfaces from assistive technology agents
Prototyping IoT haptic applications from development agents
Game/experience integration with physical feedback from entertainment agents
Developer testing of Buttplug.io protocol implementations
not_for: Teams without compatible Buttplug.io supported devices
Non-haptic IoT applications (use appropriate IoT MCPs)
best_when: A developer builds interactive experiences requiring haptic feedback and has Buttplug.io-compatible devices — integrating physical haptic control into AI agent workflows.
avoid_when: You don't have compatible devices or need other IoT control (use Home Assistant or similar).

auth.notes: No authentication — local Buttplug server connection via Bluetooth/USB. Buttplug Intiface application must be running on the host machine.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Buttplug.io is completely free and open source
pricing.notes: Buttplug.io protocol and Intiface are free open source. MCP community project is free. Device costs vary.

known_agent_gotchas: Intiface Central (Buttplug server) must be running on host machine
Bluetooth device pairing must be configured before MCP use
Device compatibility depends on Buttplug.io support list
Community MCP — niche use case, limited support
Bluetooth connectivity can be unreliable — connection errors are common
Personal use context — not appropriate for workplace/enterprise deployment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Bluetooth communication. No external credentials. Community MCP. Personal use context. Intiface server access limited to local machine.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local Bluetooth. No credentials. Community MCP. Personal use.
